old_claim.note: Integrated Circuits (Microchips) is modeled as a necessary component or method for this technology in the current graph.

new_claim_absent: No direct integrated-circuits edge remains because the more specific VLSI design node captures the implementation layer for Berkeley RISC-I while already carrying integrated-circuit ancestry.

source_shape.source_locator: IEEE ETHW identifies RISC-I as a VLSI reduced-instruction-set computer, describes MOSIS fabrication, and emphasizes single-chip VLSI implementation.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The direct implementation dependency for the scoped node is VLSI design, not the broader integrated-circuit category.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports replacing the broad integrated-circuits edge because VLSI design is the source-backed narrower prerequisite and integrated circuits remain upstream through that node.

ontology_before: The graph connected RISC microprocessors directly to broad integrated circuits.
ontology_after: The graph routes implementation through vlsi_design as the direct source-backed prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: direct risc_microprocessors -> integrated_circuits edge removed and replaced by risc_microprocessors -> vlsi_design.
would_reject_if: The VLSI design node stopped representing single-chip IC design methodology.
A future split made RISC-I a non-VLSI architectural-only node.
short_reason: VLSI design is the narrower implementation prerequisite.
